quiz_questions.question_text: In a symmetric distribution, the mean and median are:
quiz_questions.explanation: In a symmetric distribution, data is evenly distributed around the center, so the mean and median are approximately equal.

quiz_questions.question_text: A distribution that has a long tail extending to the right is called:
quiz_questions.explanation: A right-skewed distribution has a long tail extending toward higher values (to the right).

quiz_questions.question_text: In a left-skewed distribution, which relationship is typically true?
quiz_questions.explanation: In a left-skewed distribution, the tail extends to the left pulling the mean lower, so the mean is typically less than the median.

quiz_questions.question_text: A histogram shows bars of approximately equal height across all intervals. This distribution is best described as:
quiz_questions.explanation: A uniform distribution has approximately equal frequencies across all intervals, producing bars of similar height.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which type of graph is most commonly used to display the shape of a data distribution?
quiz_questions.explanation: Histograms display the frequency of data within intervals, making them ideal for showing the shape of a data distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A distribution with two distinct peaks is called:
quiz_questions.explanation: A bimodal distribution has two distinct peaks (modes), suggesting data may come from two different groups.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a right-skewed distribution, which relationship is typically true?
quiz_questions.explanation: In a right-skewed distribution, the tail extends toward higher values, pulling the mean to the right, so mean > median.

quiz_questions.question_text: A bell-shaped distribution is also known as a:
quiz_questions.explanation: A normal distribution has a symmetric, bell-shaped curve where data clusters around the mean.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which distribution shape would you expect for the ages of students in a single grade level?
quiz_questions.explanation: Students in a single grade are roughly the same age, so their ages would cluster symmetrically around a central value.

quiz_questions.question_text: If a histogram has most of its data on the left with a long tail to the right, it is:
quiz_questions.explanation: When data clusters on the left and the tail extends to the right (toward higher values), the distribution is skewed right (positively skewed).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'distribution' mean in statistics?

options.text: The average of all data values
The way data values are spread out or arranged
The method used to collect data
The number of data points collected

quiz_questions.explanation: A distribution describes how data values are spread out, including the shape, center, and spread of the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'skewed' mean when describing a distribution?

options.text: The distribution is not symmetric and has a longer tail on one side

quiz_questions.explanation: A skewed distribution is asymmetric, with data bunched on one side and a longer tail stretching toward the other side.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'histogram'?

options.text: A graph that uses bars to show the frequency of data within intervals
A graph that compares parts of a whole
A graph that shows the relationship between two variables
A graph that shows how data changes over time

quiz_questions.explanation: A histogram uses adjacent bars to show the frequency (count) of data values within consecutive, non-overlapping intervals.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'symmetric' mean when describing a data distribution?

options.text: The data has outliers on both sides
The left and right sides of the distribution are approximately mirror images
The data has exactly two peaks

quiz_questions.explanation: A symmetric distribution has a shape where the left half is approximately a mirror image of the right half when divided at the center.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'unimodal' mean?
quiz_questions.explanation: A unimodal distribution has exactly one peak (one mode), meaning data values cluster around a single central area.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'frequency' in the context of data distributions?

options.text: The number of times a data value or range of values occurs
The average of a data set
The total number of data points

quiz_questions.explanation: Frequency is the count of how many times a particular value or values within an interval appear in a data set.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'normal distribution' mean?

options.text: A distribution where all values have equal frequency
Any distribution that is not skewed
A symmetric, bell-shaped distribution where most data clusters near the mean

quiz_questions.explanation: A normal distribution is a specific symmetric, bell-shaped curve where data clusters around the mean, with about 68% of data within one standard deviation.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'uniform distribution' mean?

options.text: A distribution that is always skewed right
A distribution where every value or interval has approximately the same frequency
A distribution shaped like a bell

quiz_questions.explanation: In a uniform distribution, all values or intervals occur with roughly equal frequency, producing a flat, rectangular shape.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'gap' mean in a data distribution?

options.text: A value that appears most often
A region where no data values occur

quiz_questions.explanation: A gap in a distribution is an interval or region with no data values, which may indicate separate groups or unusual patterns in the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'cluster' mean in a data distribution?

options.text: A single outlier in the data
An area where data values are far apart
The highest value in the data set
An area where data values are grouped closely together

quiz_questions.explanation: A cluster is a group of data values that are close together, often appearing as a concentration of data points in a particular region of the distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A doctor collected the ages of 20 patients visiting a clinic in one day: 2, 5, 8, 12, 15, 22, 25, 28, 30, 35, 62, 65, 67, 68, 70, 71, 72, 74, 75, 78. What is the best description of this distribution?
quiz_questions.explanation: The data clusters into two groups (young patients under 35 and older patients over 60) with a gap in between, making this a bimodal distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A company surveyed employees about their commute times in minutes: most employees reported 10-30 minutes, but a few reported 60-90 minutes. The mean commute was 35 minutes and the median was 25 minutes. What does this suggest about the distribution?
quiz_questions.explanation: Since the mean (35) is greater than the median (25), the distribution is skewed right. The few employees with very long commutes pull the mean higher than the median.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher gave a test and most students scored between 85 and 100, but a few students scored between 40 and 55. The mean was 78 and the median was 85. Which type of distribution does this describe?
quiz_questions.explanation: Since the mean (78) is less than the median (85), the distribution is skewed left. The few low scores create a tail to the left, pulling the mean down.

quiz_questions.question_text: A store tracks the number of customers per hour over a 12-hour day: 15, 18, 22, 45, 52, 60, 58, 55, 40, 25, 20, 12. The store manager wants to describe the overall pattern. What shape does this distribution most likely have?

options.text: Approximately symmetric with a single peak

quiz_questions.explanation: Customer counts rise from 15 to a peak around 60, then fall back to 12, creating a roughly symmetric, unimodal (single-peaked) pattern.

quiz_questions.question_text: A school collected data on how many books students read over the summer. The histogram shows a peak at 0-2 books with a long tail extending to 15-20 books. The mean is 4.5 and the median is 3. Which measure of center better represents the typical student?

options.text: The range, because it shows variability
The mean, because it uses all data values
The mode, because it is the highest bar
The median, because the distribution is skewed right

quiz_questions.explanation: In a right-skewed distribution, the mean is pulled toward the tail by high values. The median is a better representative of the typical value because it is resistant to the skew.

quiz_questions.question_text: A scientist measured the lengths of 50 fish caught in a lake. The histogram shows a bell-shaped curve centered at 22 cm. If 68% of the fish are between 19 cm and 25 cm, what does this suggest about the standard deviation?

options.text: The standard deviation cannot be determined
The standard deviation is approximately 3 cm
The standard deviation is approximately 6 cm
The standard deviation is approximately 1.5 cm

quiz_questions.explanation: In a normal distribution, about 68% of data falls within one standard deviation of the mean. Since 19 to 25 spans 6 cm centered at 22, one standard deviation is 25 - 22 = 3 cm.

quiz_questions.question_text: A city surveyed household incomes. The data showed a peak around $50,000 with a long tail extending past $500,000. A reporter wants to describe the 'typical' household income. Why is the median a better choice than the mean?

options.text: The median uses all data values equally
The median is always larger than the mean
The mean cannot be calculated for income data
The median is not pulled by the extremely high incomes in the right tail

quiz_questions.explanation: Income distributions are typically skewed right by a few very high earners. The median is resistant to these extreme values and better represents a typical household income.

quiz_questions.question_text: A marathon collected finish times for 500 runners. The distribution was approximately normal with a mean of 4.5 hours. A runner finished in 3.2 hours. Where would this runner fall in the distribution?

options.text: In the center of the distribution
In the right tail, slower than most runners
In the left tail, faster than most runners
At the peak of the distribution

quiz_questions.explanation: With a mean of 4.5 hours, a finish time of 3.2 hours is below the mean, placing the runner in the left tail of the distribution (faster than most runners).

quiz_questions.question_text: A toy factory checks the weight of stuffed animals. Weights should be normally distributed around 200 grams. Quality control finds the distribution is skewed right. What might this indicate?

options.text: Some stuffed animals are being overfilled with stuffing
All stuffed animals weigh exactly 200 grams

quiz_questions.explanation: A right-skewed distribution indicates some unusually heavy items pulling the tail right, suggesting some stuffed animals are being overfilled beyond the target weight.

quiz_questions.question_text: A meteorologist collected daily high temperatures for a city over a year. The histogram shows two peaks: one around 35°F and another around 80°F. What does this bimodal distribution likely represent?

options.text: The city has consistent temperatures year-round
The two peaks represent winter and summer temperatures

quiz_questions.explanation: A bimodal temperature distribution with peaks at 35°F and 80°F likely reflects the seasonal variation between cold winter months and warm summer months.
